item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like it needs more. It ends somewhat abruptly, cutting off the immersion.
Perhaps you could add experiment logs or perhaps give documentation to explain its classification as "Euclid".

The current attachment no longer works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who thought Stargate?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

SCP-2213 is not the only extra-dimensional shenanigans the SCPF has had to deal with pouring into our world, SCP-1393 and SCP-2734 are both other examples of this. However, while the former is managed through a diligent cleanup, and the later through an misinformation campaign, the amount of people popping into our world through SCP-2213 is just too great to let these kind of containment protocols be effective, something Dr. Jacobs may comment on in his note. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

I don't have enough information to make a definitive decision on this and I don't have time to get more. That's probably the point of the containment procedures in the first place. You need to get more information and leave it here for yourself or the next guy, and hope against hope they do the right thing with it. Yeah, these guys coming through would mean the end of secrecy on this project. There's no way we could've hidden 13 million hairless cat eyed people from the world. It doesn't matter. Find out more, and if I'm right, find a way to make it stop

To this:

I don't have enough information to make a definitive decision on this and I don't have time to get more. That's probably the point of the containment procedures in the first place. You need to get more information and leave it here for yourself or the next guy, and hope against hope they do the right thing with it. Yeah, these guys coming through would mean the end of secrecy on this project. Unlike SCP-1393, which can be managed through diligent clean up, or SCP-2734, which can be controlled by a misinformation campaign, there's no way we could've hidden 13 million hairless cat eyed people from the world. It doesn't matter. Find out more, and if I'm right, find a way to make it stop

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even harsher now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: May I just say this feels particularly chilling, in the wake of the European refugee crisis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. Also, PLEASE add somewhere that a few of them have Foundation logos in their clothes, besides civilians, policemen, etc.

As i understood it, they are evacuating from some dissaster elsewhere and comming here, where they are NOT wanted. Poor lads…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who says they know it's blocked? They're sending people through some sort of trans-spatial/temporal/dimensional device. We have no way to know what happens to something that goes in but does not successfully exit. Their bodies may be vaporized or go flying off into some other dimension (for some other Foundation to find and be perplexed by).

Example: Stargate SG-1's stargate has an iris attached to it that is flush with the event horizon of the gate, meaning that anything that hits it is bounced back into the event horizon. Stargates are one way, so anything that tries to go into the receiving gate is destroyed, hence the iris destroys anything or anyone attempting to come through. Anyone still on the sending end has no way of knowing this because nothing comes back through.

So as far as the cat people may know, they are successfully evacuating their friends and family to a safe place and have no clue that they are sending them to their doom.

item.title: Re: Wrong Bowie movie
item.content:encoded: It seemed somewhat an appropriate title, and the image originally reminded me of Bowie before I was informed it was directly lifted from The Man Who Fell to Earth.

item.title: Re: Wrong Bowie movie
item.content:encoded: Cimmerian titled this "Cat People" on the main list. That's what I'm making reference to, not the image that was once used in the article itself.

item.title: Wrong Bowie movie
item.content:encoded:
That line is from the theme to that almost pornographic cat people movie he did, not Man who fell to Earth.
Come to think of it Bowie would be good for several SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am so torn on this.

On the one hand, it's a nice, simple skip that's maybe too simple and has a fairly common format screw gimmick. On the other, I actually liked the execution of the 'yes' option and lament that the site infrastructure prevents you from having a nice, clean result with that.

So I upvoted the second page and left this one alone. Make of that what you will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Used your wording. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first sentence is a comma-fest. It could easily be phrased to remove most of those commas, like this:

SCP-2213 is a door which, if uncovered, will produce an individual (referred to as an SCP-2213-1 instance) at intervals approximately two minutes apart.

Other than that, I quite liked this. It's short and sweet, pays off well and leaves me with enough questions to keep my interest piqued. +1.

some theories
– hide block

- The entities revere us as some kinds of deities and being flung across the void to their deaths is a form of ritual sacrifice, which would explain why it occurs at such consistent intervals. They know the door is closed and they're being sent to their deaths, but it's to appease us.
- The individual is the same exact specimen and he's been subjected to forever be flung to his death every two minutes for punishment for some sort of crime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been putting out the fire with GASOLINE

A little underwhelmed by this one, to be honest. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-2213: Goobacks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty great—It's like a higher-dimensional version of those mobile games where you toss ragdoll men around a stage to hit a target. The thuds against the covering break my heart. Also, I feel like a jerk for clicking "yes". :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted once I read what the vibrations were. +1 man, you deserve it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If that picture's going to stay removed, you should delete it from the page's files.

Apologies for the non-CC image. Someday, someone will have some time to go through and sort through that site's images.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: absolutely brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Is there a way to delete all my comments?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
/me looked just now

You're right. I noticed the strong resemblance to bowie and imagined it was a touched up picture. I'd grabbed it from the Visual Records site, but looking up "The man who fell to earth" I'm seeing that it looks like it may've just been lifted directly from the movie.

That's disappointing. I'll take it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice catch on the Pensacola thing, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh hey, that's David Bowie from The Man Who Fell to Earth. That's not at all jarring.

In all seriousness, the picture took me out of the experience the instant I saw it, and somehow, I highly doubt the image is CC Compliant. Change the picture and I might consider reading the other half of it. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this one. Minor nitpickery:

  • The original version doesn't mention the door being in Pensacola, while the supplement does. I assume this wasn't intentional.
  • The picture is cool but it's pretty immersion-breaking for me. It screams 1950s scifi and just doesn't work for me in the document.

Overall, still a good'un and +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tire of the "hey, the collapsible now contains a note bigger than the actual scip that explains everything directly" trope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate second-person narration or instruction. I just hate it. I also think interface screws are becoming too common, and the whole "The portal is clearly blocked but they keep suiciding themselves into it" is a bit much to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Is there a way to delete all my comments?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is horrifying. Like, this is Kurt Vonnegut Jr. levels of depressingly horrifying. And it's so simple.

+Spoilers
– hide block

This can be summarized as, "The Foundation is committing genocide for the sake of secrecy," and it is beautiful in its simplicity.


I only hope that others can appreciate the horror of this piece. +1, no question.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AH, thank you. Like multiple organs of the same type. Gotcha'.

You know what, on account of that I can't actually find any sentences with suspicious structure, I'm going to change my vote. It might just be that our diction's differ enough so that I just don't like how it's written, which doesn't mean it isn't good, it just means that, well, I don't like how it's written.

Cautious upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Humans have organs with some redundancy, Kidney's come to mind. These things just have multiple hearts, and a few other major organs which give them a resistance(but not immunity) to most weapons at least in the short term. You likely can't take out their heart, for instance, but they'll probably still bleed out.

If you spot shady grammar, please point it out so I can fix it. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, i get it.

It's well written and it's a good concept. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1: Yes the audible vibrations are the deaths of a -1 instance.

2: The numbers we're looking at are, over a 50 year period about 13 million dead instances from hitting the cover.

3: This is to ensure that the site director, who has to observe the procedures, and likely could tell that these are not brainless beasts, isn't contaminated by that information. In this case, the director deleted the note from the last director, didn't do any of the digging or investigation the old site directed hoped he would, and instead just took his amnestic treatment(which is what happens when you click "yes").

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote at the moment. I feel that i misunderstood the concept of the article, and due to that i want to really understand it before making a decision. Given that, i do have some questions.

  • So, the audible vibrations emitted by the cover only occur when it's directly over the door? If that's a yes, this vibration is supposed to be the hairless entities hitting the door and being knocked back, and then dying, right? Do they die from the impact or is there a second cause?
  • When the author of the note says:

"There's no way we could've hidden 13 million hairless cat eyed people from the world."

Is he making an estimate of what could have happened or is he making a statement about something that has happened? And, also, if that actually happened (the gradative releasing of the cat-eyed organisms), how did the termination upon the replacing of the cover fail?

  • The fact that the higher command gives the Site Director amnestics after the execution of the procedure: Is this a way to fully conceal the true nature of the entities, should the Director read the note from the previous one?

Thank you in advance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-2213: David Bowie

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thought I broke the skip at the end there, for a minute. Nice touch.

Going to no-vote on this one. It's clear that a ton effort is present here, but there's some shady grammars here and there, and I have no idea what

redundant organs

are, so if that is actually a thing, I apologize in advance for my absent knowledge of said thing.. The idea of inter-dimensional refugees seems rather cliched, but I like ("like" is subjective here) how the Foundation essentially has no other choice but to execute them all to prevent a breach of secrecy.

EDIT: Also, that's a remarkably good picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: E: On second though ignore this post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok. So this is a thing. There's a secondary page to this as well, which tells the rest of the story, sort of. The image is from the visual records site. Thanks to all the folks on IRC who helped me work out the kinks in this.
